subject: Databases
title: Partial Indexes
description: Learn Partial Indexes in one minute.

hook.text: Only 2% of orders are 'pending'. A full index on status wastes space on the 98% that are 'completed'.

buildup.text: A partial index includes only rows that match a condition: CREATE INDEX ON orders(id) WHERE status = 'pending'.

discovery.text: The index is tiny. It covers only the rows you actually query. Faster to scan, less RAM used.

twist.text: The query's WHERE clause must match the index condition. Otherwise the planner ignores the partial index.

climax.text: Partial indexes are perfect for status flags, soft deletes, and active/inactive filters.

punchline.text: Index only what you query. Partial indexes save space and speed.

quiz.question: When is a partial index most beneficial?
options: When queries only target a small subset of rows, like active records or pending orders
When the table has fewer than 100 rows
Partial indexes are always better than full indexes
